reviews.review_description:
It's asking me for an access code provided by a hospital. I don't understand why they're launching an app on a meta store that reaches millions of users if they only wanted to release an app for fewer than 50 people worldwide.
I was a medical student at university, I did my internship and social service, I'm in the process of getting my degree, and I think it's also important to give future surgical residents or interns currently in hospitals the opportunity to try these apps. They may not be operating right now, but by going into a real operating room they can better understand what's happening.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for sharing your feedback. We understand your frustration, and we appreciate your interest in the app and in this type of surgical support technology.

Access is protected by username and password because the app is designed for controlled professional use, in line with current legal and traceability requirements applicable to these medical workflows. This means it is not available for open access without the appropriate credentials.

We especially value your point about training and education. In fact, this application has been co-developed with surgeons who work in real operating room environments, precisely to support better understanding and decision-making in clinical practice. If you would like to formally request access to demo or testing content, our support mailbox is open at support@rayo-seco.com

Thank you again for your interest and for your thoughtful comment.
